beats.activity: Drive from Leschi to Capitol Hill for work
beats.reasoning: Light Rail closure between Westlake and U-District (9am–3pm) doesn't affect the Leschi–Capitol Hill route; Andre drives to avoid any transit uncertainty.

beats.location_name: Capitol Hill Elementary School (Capitol Hill, Seattle)

beats.activity: Prepare dinner and eat on the back patio, enjoying the warm evening

beats.activity: Work on a pottery piece in the home studio; Read a poetry collection and prepare for bed

Restful sleep after a balanced day of work, leisure, and creative time; Andre wakes refreshed for Tuesday.

diary:
Woke up to that perfect early June light streaming through the bedroom—the kind that makes you remember why you stay in Seattle. Had my usual coffee routine, then drove up to Capitol Hill through light traffic. The morning sessions went well; one of my fourth-graders finally opened up about the bullying stuff we've been working through, which felt like a real win.

I couldn't resist ducking into Serious Pie around ten to catch the USA match. The place was packed already, everyone buzzing about it. Grabbed a sandwich and watched the first half before heading back to school—felt a little guilty sneaking out but also energized by the crowd's energy.

After work, I drove downtown on a whim. Pike Place was gorgeous in the afternoon sun. Spent nearly an hour talking with this old vendor about his poetry books—he recommended something I'd never heard of. Drove home feeling unhurried for once.

Dinner on the patio was perfect. Then lost myself in the studio with clay, finally getting that bowl shape right. Ended the day with poetry and quiet. A good Monday.
thought_process: The World Cup timing actually pulled me toward downtown instead of heading straight home—I haven't felt that kind of casual social energy in weeks, and I let myself lean into it rather than stick rigidly to routine. The Light Rail closure didn't affect my route, which was lucky. I think the sunshine and the match atmosphere shifted something in my mood; I went from my usual structured workday into something more exploratory. The Pike Place detour felt spontaneous but necessary—I've been in counseling mode so much that talking to a stranger about books reminded me I'm more than just the person listening to other people's problems. By evening I was genuinely settled, not just tired.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
